SEARCH
Package neqsim.util.nucleation

Class AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.nucleation.AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance
public class AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance extends Object
Predicts the attainable metastability (superheat / pressure-undershoot limit) of a depressurising liquid using the volume balancing method.

When a liquid is depressurised rapidly (for example the rarefaction wave that travels into a pipe after a full-bore rupture), phase change is delayed and the liquid becomes metastable (superheated) below its saturation pressure. Classical nucleation theory (CNT, see ClassicalNucleationTheory) reproduces the attainable superheat near the critical point but over-predicts it strongly at low reduced temperature, where an empirical, temperature-dependent correction factor is normally required.

The volume balancing method removes that empirical factor by recognising that, away from the critical point, the limit is set by evaporation into pre-existing bubbles (e.g. gas pockets trapped in wall crevices) rather than by homogeneous nucleation. The metastability limit is the point on the depressurisation path where the rate of vapour volume created by the growth of those bubbles first balances the rate of liquid volume lost through the rarefaction outflow: 

   dV/dt|_evaporation  >=  dV/dt|_outflow      (metastability limit reached)

Volume loss (outflow). The depressurising liquid is treated as an isentropic rarefaction wave. The outflow speed u is integrated along the metastable isentrope from the initial pressure using the Riemann invariant for a simple wave, 

   u_i = u_{i-1} - 2 (p_{i-1} - p_i) / (rho_i c_i + rho_{i-1} c_{i-1})
   dV/dt|_outflow = -u_i * A_pipe

where c is the single-phase (metastable) liquid speed of sound and A_pipe the pipe cross-sectional area.

Volume creation (bubble growth). Each pre-existing bubble grows by heat-transfer-limited evaporation following the asymptotic Plesset & Zwick (1954) solution (Collier & Thome, Convective Boiling and Condensation, Eq. 4.27). Evaluated at a short reference time dt the bubble radius and growth rate are 

   R    = 2 dTsat k_l / (h_lg rho_g) * sqrt(3 dt / (pi alpha_l))
   dR/dt = 0.5 R / dt
   dV/dt|_evaporation = 4 pi R^2 (dR/dt) * n_bub

with liquid superheat dTsat = T - Tsat(p), liquid thermal conductivity k_l, thermal diffusivity alpha_l = k_l/(rho_l c_pl), latent heat h_lg, saturated vapour density rho_g, and a single temperature-independent tuning parameter n_bub (the number of pre-existing bubbles).

All thermodynamic and transport properties are taken from the supplied NeqSim SystemInterface, so any equation of state available in NeqSim (SRK, PR, GERG-2008, Span-Wagner type reference EOS, ...) can be used. The metastable liquid and vapour roots are obtained with SystemInterface.setForceSinglePhase(PhaseType). Unlike the original reference implementation, the liquid thermal conductivity is taken from NeqSim's general transport property models rather than a substance-specific correlation, so the method is not limited to a single fluid.

Usage: 

 
 SystemInterface co2 = new SystemSrkEos(273.15, 120.0);
 co2.addComponent("CO2", 1.0);
 co2.setMixingRule("classic");

 AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance vb = new AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance(co2);
 vb.setNumberOfBubbles(1.0e8);
 vb.setPipeRadius(0.02);
 vb.calculateLimit(273.15, 120.0e5); // T0 [K], p0 [Pa]

 if (vb.isLimitFound()) {
   double pLimitBar = vb.getLimitPressure() / 1e5;
   double superheat = vb.getSuperheat(); // K above saturation
 }

Scope: screening / research method for a pure (or pseudo-pure) liquid. It predicts the limit of metastability, not the subsequent two-phase flashing flow. For the equilibrium blowdown driving force use DepressurizationSimulator; for the metastable / spinodal region classification use SpinodalDecompositionDetector.

References:

  • Log, A.M. (2025). On the attainable metastability of liquids during depressurisation - effect of pre-existing bubbles. Journal of Fluid Mechanics 1018, R2. https://doi.org/10.1017/jfm.2025.10545
  • Plesset, M.S. and Zwick, S.A. (1954). The growth of vapor bubbles in superheated liquids. J. Appl. Phys. 25, 493-500.
  • Collier, J.G. and Thome, J.R. (1994). Convective Boiling and Condensation, 3rd ed., Eq. 4.27.
Version:
1.0
Author:
esol

  • Field Details

    • logger

      private static final org.apache.logging.log4j.Logger logger
      Logger.

    • fluid

      private final SystemInterface fluid
      The NeqSim fluid (pure or pseudo-pure) supplied by the caller.

    • numberOfBubbles

      private double numberOfBubbles
      Number of pre-existing bubbles (free tuning parameter). Default 1.0e8.

    • pipeRadius

      private double pipeRadius
      Pipe radius in m (sets the outflow cross-sectional area). Default 0.02 m.

    • minimumPressure

      private double minimumPressure
      Lower pressure bound of the depressurisation search in Pa. Default 3.0e5 Pa.

    • numberOfSteps

      private int numberOfSteps
      Number of pressure steps used to integrate the isentrope. Default 1000.

    • bubbleReferenceTime

      private double bubbleReferenceTime
      Reference time for the asymptotic Plesset-Zwick growth evaluation in s. Default 1.0e-4 s.

    • initialTemperature

      private double initialTemperature
      Initial temperature in K.

    • initialPressure

      private double initialPressure
      Initial pressure in Pa.

    • limitFound

      private boolean limitFound
      Whether the metastability limit was located within the search range.

    • limitPressure

      private double limitPressure
      Pressure at the metastability limit in Pa.

    • limitTemperature

      private double limitTemperature
      Temperature at the metastability limit in K.

    • limitSaturationTemperature

      private double limitSaturationTemperature
      Saturation temperature at the limit pressure in K.

    • superheat

      private double superheat
      Liquid superheat (T - Tsat) at the limit in K.

    • calculated

      private boolean calculated
      Whether calculateLimit(double, double) has been run.

    • message

      private String message
      Human-readable status message.

  • Constructor Details

    • AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance

      public AttainableMetastabilityVolumeBalance(SystemInterface fluid)
      Creates a volume balancing metastability solver for the given fluid.

      The fluid should contain a single component (or a pseudo-pure mixture) and have its mixing rule set. The fluid is cloned internally, so the caller's object is not modified.

      Parameters:
      fluid - the NeqSim thermodynamic system to use as the property source

  • Method Details

    • setNumberOfBubbles

      public void setNumberOfBubbles(double numberOfBubbles)
      Sets the number of pre-existing bubbles (the single temperature-independent tuning parameter).
      Parameters:
      numberOfBubbles - number of pre-existing bubbles (must be positive)

    • setPipeRadius

      public void setPipeRadius(double pipeRadius)
      Sets the pipe radius that defines the outflow cross-sectional area.
      Parameters:
      pipeRadius - pipe radius in m (must be positive)

    • setMinimumPressure

      public void setMinimumPressure(double minimumPressure)
      Sets the lower pressure bound of the depressurisation search.
      Parameters:
      minimumPressure - minimum pressure in Pa

    • setNumberOfSteps

      public void setNumberOfSteps(int numberOfSteps)
      Sets the number of pressure steps used to integrate the isentrope.
      Parameters:
      numberOfSteps - number of integration steps (must be > 1)

    • setBubbleReferenceTime

      public void setBubbleReferenceTime(double bubbleReferenceTime)
      Sets the reference time used to evaluate the asymptotic Plesset-Zwick bubble growth rate.
      Parameters:
      bubbleReferenceTime - reference time in s (must be positive)

    • calculateLimit

      public void calculateLimit(double initialTemperatureK, double initialPressurePa)
      Calculates the attainable metastability limit for the given initial liquid state.

      The liquid is depressurised isentropically from (initialTemperatureK, initialPressurePa) down to the minimum pressure. Along the metastable isentrope the outflow rate and the bubble-growth rate are compared at every step; the limit is the first point below saturation where bubble growth balances the outflow.

      Parameters:
      initialTemperatureK - initial (liquid) temperature in K
      initialPressurePa - initial pressure in Pa

    • solveIsentropicLiquidTemperature

      private double solveIsentropicLiquidTemperature(SystemInterface liquid, ThermodynamicOperations ops, double pressurePa, double entropyTarget, double temperatureGuess)
      Solves the metastable single-phase liquid temperature on the reference isentrope at a given pressure using a secant iteration on molar entropy.
      Parameters:
      liquid - the forced single-phase liquid clone
      ops - thermodynamic operations bound to liquid
      pressurePa - pressure in Pa
      entropyTarget - target molar entropy in J/(mol K)
      temperatureGuess - initial temperature guess in K
      Returns:
      the isentropic liquid temperature in K

    • saturationTemperature

      private double saturationTemperature(SystemInterface saturation, ThermodynamicOperations ops, double pressurePa, double temperatureGuess)
      Computes the saturation (bubble point) temperature of the fluid at a given pressure.
      Parameters:
      saturation - the saturation clone (normal phase behaviour)
      ops - thermodynamic operations bound to saturation
      pressurePa - pressure in Pa
      temperatureGuess - starting temperature guess in K
      Returns:
      saturation temperature in K

    • bubbleGrowthVolumeRate

      private double bubbleGrowthVolumeRate(SystemInterface liquid, SystemInterface vapour, ThermodynamicOperations vapourOps, double pressurePa, double temperatureK, double superheatK)
      Computes the rate of vapour volume creation from heat-transfer-limited growth of the pre-existing bubbles (asymptotic Plesset-Zwick solution).
      Parameters:
      liquid - the forced single-phase liquid clone, already flashed to (T, p)
      vapour - the forced single-phase vapour clone
      vapourOps - thermodynamic operations bound to vapour
      pressurePa - pressure in Pa
      temperatureK - metastable liquid temperature in K
      superheatK - liquid superheat (T - Tsat) in K
      Returns:
      volume creation rate in m^3/s (0 if growth is not physical)

    • isLimitFound

      public boolean isLimitFound()
      Returns whether the metastability limit was located within the search range.
      Returns:
      true if a limit was found

    • getLimitPressure

      public double getLimitPressure()
      Returns the pressure at the attainable metastability limit.
      Returns:
      limit pressure in Pa (NaN if not found)

    • getLimitTemperature

      public double getLimitTemperature()
      Returns the temperature at the attainable metastability limit.
      Returns:
      limit temperature in K (NaN if not found)

    • getLimitSaturationTemperature

      public double getLimitSaturationTemperature()
      Returns the saturation temperature at the limit pressure.
      Returns:
      saturation temperature in K (NaN if not found)

    • getSuperheat

      public double getSuperheat()
      Returns the liquid superheat (T - Tsat) at the metastability limit.
      Returns:
      superheat in K (NaN if not found)

    • getInitialTemperature

      public double getInitialTemperature()
      Returns the initial temperature used for the calculation.
      Returns:
      initial temperature in K

    • getInitialPressure

      public double getInitialPressure()
      Returns the initial pressure used for the calculation.
      Returns:
      initial pressure in Pa

    • getNumberOfBubbles

      public double getNumberOfBubbles()
      Returns the number of pre-existing bubbles used as the tuning parameter.
      Returns:
      number of bubbles

    • isCalculated

      public boolean isCalculated()
      Returns whether calculateLimit(double, double) has been run.
      Returns:
      true if calculated

    • getMessage

      public String getMessage()
      Returns a human-readable status message describing the outcome.
      Returns:
      status message

    • toMap

      public Map<String,Object> toMap()
      Returns all results as an ordered map for serialization.
      Returns:
      map of result names to values

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Returns the results as a pretty-printed JSON string.
      Returns:
      JSON representation of the results
